dashboard.title: My patients
dashboard.invite_card_subtitle: The patient will receive a link by email to create their account and access the app.
dashboard.invite_birth_date_error_future: Date cannot be in the future.
dashboard.patient_already_registered: This patient is already in your list.
dashboard.invitation_already_pending: An invitation is already pending for this email.
dashboard.invite_module_intro: Optional — these modules will be automatically unlocked when the patient registers.
dashboard.invite_module_config_note: Psychoeducation and RIM require configuration from the patient page and are not available here.
dashboard.empty_description: Start by inviting your first patient.
dashboard.empty_search_description: No patient matches your search. Check the spelling or clear the field to see all.

full_title.rcads: Revised Children's Anxiety and Depression Scale
full_title.asrs18: Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale v1.1 — Full Assessment (18 items)
full_title.asrs6: Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale v1.1 — Screener (6 items)
full_title.snap_iv: Swanson, Nolan and Pelham Rating Scale – version IV

descriptions.phq9: 9-item depression screening and severity questionnaire. Global reference for longitudinal follow-up.
descriptions.gad7: 7-item rapid assessment of generalized anxiety disorder. Sensitive to therapeutic change.
descriptions.epds: Perinatal depression screening (antenatal and postnatal). International reference recommended by NICE.
descriptions.rcads: Measures anxious and depressive symptoms in children and adolescents. Self-report and parent versions available.
descriptions.bsl23: Measures severity of borderline personality disorder symptoms. Longitudinal monitoring tool in DBT.
descriptions.cape42: Assessment of subclinical positive, negative and depressive experiences in general and clinical populations.
descriptions.audit: WHO 10-item screening for problematic alcohol use. Recommended for early detection in primary care.
descriptions.nsi: 9-item multidimensional nightmare severity assessment (score 0–45). Measures wake frequency, emotional intensity and daytime impact.
descriptions.asrs18: Full ADHD self-report (18 items, Parts A+B, ASRS v1.1). Total score 0–72, Part A 0–24, Part B 0–48.
descriptions.asrs6: 6-item ADHD screening (Part A, ASRS v1.1). Total score 0–24. WHO-validated for rapid detection.
descriptions.snap_iv: 26-item behavioural ADHD screening (18 DSM-IV + 8 ODD criteria). Clinician-rated by parent or teacher.
descriptions.cssrs: Structured assessment of suicidal ideation and behaviour. Columbia University / NIMH — 'Since last visit' version.

patient.empty_tools: No tools unlocked — use the therapeutic wardrobe below.
patient.wardrobe_desc: Click a category to see available tools and unlock them for this patient.
patient.coming_soon: Module under development — preview not available.

patient.psycho_pick_edit: Edit unlocked cards for this patient:

patient.rim_orig_placeholder: Description of the original nightmare, for reference...
patient.crisis_msg_label: Support message (shown to patient in their reasons to hold on)
patient.crisis_msg_placeholder: An encouraging word, a supportive thought...
patient.crisis_commitment_placeholder: E.g. I commit to contacting my carer before any act...
patient.crisis_card_thought_placeholder: E.g. I am a burden to everyone...
patient.crisis_card_response_placeholder: E.g. Those who love me have said otherwise...
patient.overview_no_modules: No modules unlocked for this patient.
patient.rdv_coming_soon: Appointment management will be available soon.
patient.sources_intro: Sources verified via PubMed and official health organisation websites.
patient.sources_empty: No sources available for this module.
patient.cssrs_modal_subtitle: Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale · Clinician-rated

modules.title: Module Library
modules.subtitle: Enable the modules available for your patients. Only enabled modules can be unlocked from a patient's profile.

phq9.label: PHQ-9
phq9.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first PHQ-9.
phq9.instructions_2: how often have you been bothered by any of the following problems?
phq9.q1: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
phq9.q3: Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
phq9.q4: Feeling tired or having little energy
phq9.q6: Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
phq9.q7: Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
phq9.q8: Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed. Or the opposite — being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
phq9.q9: Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way
phq9.footer: The score is shared with your clinician. Interpretation is their responsibility.

gad7.label: GAD-7
gad7.description: 7-item generalized anxiety questionnaire (Spitzer et al., 2006). Score 0–21.
gad7.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first GAD-7.
gad7.instructions_2: how often have you been bothered by the following problems?
gad7.q1: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
gad7.q2: Not being able to stop or control worrying
gad7.q3: Worrying too much about different things
gad7.q5: Being so restless that it's hard to sit still
gad7.q7: Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen
gad7.footer: The score is shared with your clinician. Interpretation is their responsibility.

bsl23.label: BSL-23
bsl23.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first BSL-23.
bsl23.instructions_2: how much have you suffered from the following problems?
bsl23.q3: Feeling uncomfortable in my own skin
bsl23.q5: Feeling like I'm outside my own body
bsl23.q8: Being unable to express my feelings
bsl23.q9: Thinking about hurting or injuring myself
bsl23.q10: Feeling as though I don't exist
bsl23.q12: Feeling so bad that I want to hurt myself
bsl23.q13: Feeling so tense that I feel like I'm going to explode
bsl23.q14: Feeling agitated and unable to calm down
bsl23.q18: Finding it hard to believe that others respect me
bsl23.q20: Feeling angry and irritable for no apparent reason
bsl23.q21: Feeling that others see me negatively
bsl23.footer: The score is shared with your clinician. Interpretation is their responsibility.

snap_iv.label: SNAP-IV
snap_iv.description: ADHD screening for children/adolescents — 26 items, 3 subscales.
snap_iv.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first SNAP-IV.
snap_iv.instructions_1: Hetero-evaluation — to be filled in by a parent or teacher.
snap_iv.instructions_2: For each of the following behaviours, indicate how often you observe it in the child.
snap_iv.warning: This questionnaire must be completed by a parent, guardian or teacher — not by the child.
snap_iv.q1: Fails to give close attention to details or makes careless mistakes in schoolwork, work, or other activities.
snap_iv.q2: Has difficulty sustaining attention in tasks or play activities.
snap_iv.q3: Does not seem to listen when spoken to directly.
snap_iv.q4: Does not follow through on instructions and fails to finish schoolwork, chores, or duties (not due to oppositional behaviour or failure to understand).
snap_iv.q5: Has difficulty organising tasks and activities.
snap_iv.q6: Avoids, dislikes, or is reluctant to engage in tasks that require sustained mental effort.
snap_iv.q7: Loses things necessary for tasks or activities (pencils, books, tools, assignments).
snap_iv.q8: Is easily distracted by extraneous stimuli.
snap_iv.q10: Fidgets with hands or feet or squirms in seat.
snap_iv.q11: Leaves seat in classroom or in other situations in which remaining seated is expected.
snap_iv.q12: Runs about or climbs excessively in situations in which it is inappropriate.
snap_iv.q13: Has difficulty playing or engaging in leisure activities quietly.
snap_iv.q14: Is 'on the go' or often acts as if 'driven by a motor'.
snap_iv.q16: Blurts out answers before questions have been completed.
snap_iv.q18: Interrupts or intrudes on others (butts into conversations or games).
snap_iv.q21: Actively defies or refuses to comply with adults' requests or rules.
snap_iv.q23: Blames others for his or her mistakes or misbehaviour.
snap_iv.q24: Is touchy or easily annoyed by others.
snap_iv.footer: The score is shared with your clinician. Interpretation is their responsibility.

asrs6.label: ASRS v1.1 — Screener
asrs6.description: Adult ADHD rapid screening — 6-item Part A (Kessler et al., 2005).
asrs6.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first ASRS-6.
asrs6.instructions_2: for each of the following questions, select the option that best describes how you have felt and conducted yourself.
asrs6.q1: How often do you have difficulty wrapping up the final details of a project, once the challenging parts have been done?
asrs6.q2: How often do you have difficulty getting things in order when you have to do a task that requires organisation?
asrs6.q3: How often do you have problems remembering appointments or obligations?
asrs6.q4: When you have a task that requires a lot of thought, how often do you avoid or delay getting started?
asrs6.q5: How often do you fidget or squirm with your hands or feet when you have to sit down for a long time?
asrs6.q6: How often do you feel overly active and compelled to do things, like you were driven by a motor?
asrs6.footer: The score is shared with your clinician. Interpretation is their responsibility.

asrs18.label: ASRS v1.1 — Full Assessment
asrs18.description: Full adult ADHD assessment — 18 items Parts A+B (Kessler et al., 2005).
asrs18.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first ASRS-18.
asrs18.instructions_2: for each of the following questions, select the option that best describes how you have felt and conducted yourself.
asrs18.q1: How often do you have difficulty wrapping up the final details of a project, once the challenging parts have been done?
asrs18.q2: How often do you have difficulty getting things in order when you have to do a task that requires organisation?
asrs18.q3: How often do you have problems remembering appointments or obligations?
asrs18.q4: When you have a task that requires a lot of thought, how often do you avoid or delay getting started?
asrs18.q5: How often do you fidget or squirm with your hands or feet when you have to sit down for a long time?
asrs18.q6: How often do you feel overly active and compelled to do things, like you were driven by a motor?
asrs18.q7: How often do you make careless mistakes when you have to work on a boring or difficult project?
asrs18.q8: How often do you have difficulty keeping your attention when you are doing boring or repetitive work?
asrs18.q9: How often do you have difficulty concentrating on what people say to you, even when they are speaking to you directly?
asrs18.q10: How often do you misplace or have difficulty finding things at home or at work?
asrs18.q11: How often are you distracted by activity or noise around you?
asrs18.q12: How often do you leave your seat in meetings or other situations in which you are expected to remain seated?
asrs18.q13: How often do you feel restless or fidgety?
asrs18.q14: How often do you have difficulty unwinding and relaxing when you have time to yourself?
asrs18.q15: How often do you find yourself talking too much when you are in social situations?
asrs18.q16: When you're in a conversation, how often do you find yourself finishing the sentences of the people you are talking to, before they can finish them themselves?
asrs18.q17: How often do you have difficulty waiting your turn in situations when turn-taking is required?
asrs18.q18: How often do you interrupt others when they are busy?
asrs18.footer: The score is shared with your clinician. Interpretation is their responsibility.

cape42.label: CAPE-42 — Psychotic Experiences
cape42.description: Community Assessment of Psychic Experiences (42 items).

audit.label: AUDIT — Alcohol Screening
audit.description: Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test — WHO (10 items).

cssrs.label: C-SSRS — Suicide Risk
cssrs.description: Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale — clinician-administered.

crisis_plan.label: Crisis Plan
crisis_plan.description: Personalized action plan for difficult moments (Stanley & Brown).
crisis_plan.empty_text: Use the button above to create your crisis plan.
crisis_plan.step_1_hint: How do I feel when a crisis is approaching?
crisis_plan.step_2_hint: What can I do on my own to calm down?
crisis_plan.step_3_hint: Who can I see or where can I go to distract myself?
crisis_plan.step_4_hint: Who can listen and help me if I feel at risk?
crisis_plan.step_5_hint: Which professionals or services can I contact?
crisis_plan.step_6_hint: How can I make my surroundings safer?
crisis_plan.footer: Permanent emergency buttons at the bottom of the screen: 15 — SAMU | 3114 — Suicide Prevention
crisis_plan.urgency_subtitle: Here are your emergency contacts and resources.
crisis_plan.urgency_no_items: No contacts filled in your plan.
crisis_plan.anchors_subtitle: Images and a phrase that remind you why life is worth living.
crisis_plan.anchors_preview_info: The patient adds up to 3 photos and a personal phrase on their phone.
crisis_plan.coping_cards_subtitle: Personalised strategies prepared with your practitioner.
crisis_plan.coping_cards_empty: No cards configured — add some in the editor above.
crisis_plan.commitment_phrase_default: I commit to using this plan before any act of self-harm, and to calling my practitioner if I don't feel capable.

therapeutic_commitment.label: Therapeutic Commitment
therapeutic_commitment.description: Therapeutic alliance: commitment to using resources before any crisis act.
therapeutic_commitment.empty_text: Use the button above to fill in your therapeutic commitment.

distress_tolerance.label: Distress Tolerance Toolkit
distress_tolerance.description: DBT-inspired emergency physiological action cards for breaking acute crises.
distress_tolerance.empty_text: Use the button above to access the toolkit.

medication_side_effects.label: Treatment side effects tracker
medication_side_effects.description: Daily self-assessment (0-3): drowsiness, restlessness, tremors, dry mouth.
medication_side_effects.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first report.
medication_side_effects.events_hint: These milestones appear on the patient's chart (e.g. dose change, new medication). Neutral display — no interpretation.

medication_adherence.label: Medication Adherence Module
medication_adherence.empty_text: Use the button above to complete your first report.

psychoeducation.label: Psychoeducation
psychoeducation.description: Therapeutic knowledge cards: sleep, grounding, distortions, drug interactions.
psychoeducation.empty_text: Use the button above to browse the psychoeducation cards.

sleep_diary.label: Sleep Diary
sleep_diary.description: Daily sleep tracking: bedtime, wake time, sleep efficiency.
sleep_diary.footer: CBT-I formula (Morin, 1993) — SE (%) = Actual Sleep Time ÷ Time in Bed × 100. Actual sleep time = time in bed − sleep onset latency − total night wake duration. Reference thresholds: ≥ 85% · 70–84% · < 70%.
sleep_diary.empty_text: Use the button above to log your first night.

diet_weight_psycho.label: Diet & Weight Psychoeducation
diet_weight_psycho.description: Understanding and managing cravings and weight gain on antipsychotics.
diet_weight_psycho.empty_text: Use the button above to browse the cards.

chronobiology_tracker.label: Chronobiology Regularity Tracker
chronobiology_tracker.description: Track zeitgebers: wake time, bedtime, first meal — essential for bipolar disorders.
chronobiology_tracker.empty_text: Use the button above to log your first entry.

mood_tracker.label: Mood & Energy Thermometer
mood_tracker.description: Daily tracking of mood and energy level on a visual scale.
mood_tracker.title: Mood thermometer
mood_tracker.intro: Log your mood, energy and anxiety for today.
mood_tracker.already_saved: Today's entry already saved — you can edit it.
mood_tracker.history_empty: History will appear once you have at least 2 entries.
mood_tracker.instructions: Rate each dimension for the present moment.
mood_tracker.empty_text: Record your first entry to see your history.
mood_tracker.footer: Raw figures reported by the patient. To be interpreted in consultation.
mood_tracker.reminder_none: No reminder configured by your therapist.
mood_tracker.markers_empty: No markers in this period. Mark an event (medication change, life event…) to place it on your charts.
mood_tracker.markers_placeholder: E.g.: started lithium, time off work…
mood_tracker.month_legend: The higher the day's average score, the darker the circle (1 = light, 10 = dark).

emotion_wheel.label: Emotion Wheel
emotion_wheel.description: Explore and precisely name emotions to address alexithymia.
emotion_wheel.empty_text: Use the button above to log your first entry.

behavioral_activation.label: Behavioral Activation Journal
behavioral_activation.description: Activity planning rated by Pleasure (0-10) and Mastery (0-10).
behavioral_activation.empty_text: Use the button above to log your first activity.

beck_columns.label: Beck Columns
beck_columns.description: Thought journal: Situation, Emotion, Automatic Thought, Alternative Thought.
beck_columns.empty_text: Use the button above to log your first thought record.

cognitive_distortions.label: Cognitive Distortions Detector
cognitive_distortions.description: Educational cards on thinking traps (all-or-nothing, overgeneralization, mind-reading…)
cognitive_distortions.empty_text: Use the button above to browse the cards.

grounding.label: Grounding Techniques
grounding.description: 5-4-3-2-1 exercise to return to the present during dissociation or severe rumination.
grounding.empty_text: Use the button above to start your first grounding session.

rim.label: RIM – Mental Imagery Reprocessing
rim.footer: Mental Imagery Reprocessing (RIM) — Krakow et al., JAMA 2001 · Schmid et al., Psychother Psychosom 2021. Alternative scenario configured by the clinician, read-only for the patient.
rim.empty_text: Use the button above to start your first session.

fear_thermometer.label: Fear Thermometer
fear_thermometer.description: Measure and visualize your anxiety level (SUDs, 0-100).
fear_thermometer.empty_text: Use the button above to log your first entry.

exposure_hierarchy.label: Exposure Hierarchy
exposure_hierarchy.description: Graduated exposure step builder for phobias and OCD.
exposure_hierarchy.empty_text: Use the button above to build your first exposure hierarchy.

breathing_techniques.label: Breathing Techniques
breathing_techniques.description: Visual guide to box breathing and cardiac coherence with local counter.
breathing_techniques.empty_text: Use the button above to start your first session.

cognitive_saturation.label: Cognitive Saturation
cognitive_saturation.description: Rapid repetition exercise to interrupt ruminations.
cognitive_saturation.empty_text: Use the button above to start your first session.

craving_journal.label: Craving Journal
craving_journal.description: Log the urge intensity (0-10), the trigger, and the avoidance strategy used.
craving_journal.empty_text: Use the button above to log your first entry.

decisional_balance.label: Decisional Balance
decisional_balance.description: Motivational interviewing tool: weighing the pros and cons of a behaviour change.
decisional_balance.empty_text: Use the button above to fill in your first decisional balance.

motivational_balance.label: Motivational Balance
motivational_balance.description: Motivational Interviewing (Miller & Rollnick): Prochaska stages, importance/confidence rulers 0-10, values balance.

crisis_plan.label: Crisis Plan
crisis_plan.description: Personalized action plan for difficult moments (Stanley & Brown).
crisis_plan.step_1_hint: How do I feel when a crisis is approaching?
crisis_plan.step_2_hint: What can I do on my own to calm down?
crisis_plan.step_3_hint: Who can I see or where can I go to distract myself?
crisis_plan.step_4_hint: Who can listen and help me if I feel at risk?
crisis_plan.step_5_hint: Which professionals or services can I contact?
crisis_plan.step_6_hint: How can I make my surroundings safer?
crisis_plan.footer: Permanent emergency buttons at the bottom of the screen: 15 — SAMU | 3114 — Suicide Prevention

therapeutic_commitment.label: Therapeutic Commitment
therapeutic_commitment.description: Therapeutic alliance: commitment to using resources before any crisis act.

distress_tolerance.label: Distress Tolerance Toolkit
distress_tolerance.description: DBT-inspired emergency physiological action cards for breaking acute crises.

medication_side_effects.label: Treatment side effects tracker
medication_side_effects.description: Daily self-assessment (0-3): drowsiness, restlessness, tremors, dry mouth.
medication_side_effects.footer: Scale inspired by the UKU Side Effect Rating Scale (Lingjaerde et al., 1987). 0 = Absent · 1 = Mild · 2 = Moderate · 3 = Severe. Raw values as reported by the patient. No interpretive thresholds — MDR 2017/745 compliance. Last 30 days of history available in the app.

medication_adherence.label: Medication Adherence Module

field_5.label: History
field_5.detail: Raw list of the last 30 days (date, status, note)

medication_adherence.footer: Data stored locally on the patient's phone. The patient self-reports their status each day — no algorithmic interpretation (MDR 2017/745 compliance). Last 30 days of history available in the app.

psychoeducation.label: Psychoeducation
psychoeducation.description: Therapeutic knowledge cards: sleep, grounding, distortions, drug interactions.

sleep_diary.label: Sleep Diary
sleep_diary.description: Daily sleep tracking: bedtime, wake time, sleep efficiency.
sleep_diary.footer: CBT-I formula (Morin, 1993) — SE (%) = Actual Sleep Time ÷ Time in Bed × 100. Actual sleep time = time in bed − sleep onset latency − total night wake duration. Reference thresholds: ≥ 85% · 70–84% · < 70%.

diet_weight_psycho.label: Diet & Weight Psychoeducation
diet_weight_psycho.description: Understanding and managing cravings and weight gain on antipsychotics.

chronobiology_tracker.label: Chronobiology Regularity Tracker
chronobiology_tracker.description: Track zeitgebers: wake time, bedtime, first meal — essential for bipolar disorders.

mood_tracker.label: Mood & Energy Thermometer
mood_tracker.description: Daily tracking of mood and energy level on a visual scale.
mood_tracker.footer: Single daily entry. History available with mini-charts per dimension (last 30 days). Data stored locally on the device.

emotion_wheel.label: Emotion Wheel
emotion_wheel.description: Explore and precisely name emotions to address alexithymia.

behavioral_activation.label: Behavioral Activation Journal
behavioral_activation.description: Activity planning rated by Pleasure (0-10) and Mastery (0-10).
behavioral_activation.footer: The patient plans and rates activities on two dimensions (P and M). Data stored locally. No global score — raw P and M values are reviewed by the clinician during sessions to guide motivational interviewing.

beck_columns.label: Beck Columns
beck_columns.description: Thought journal: Situation, Emotion, Automatic Thought, Alternative Thought.

step_1.title: Situation
step_1.hint: Where was I? What was happening? Who was present?

step_2.title: Emotion(s)
step_2.hint: What emotion did I feel? How intense was it (0–100)?

step_3.title: Automatic Thought
step_3.hint: What thought came to mind? How much did I believe it (0–100)?

step_4.title: Rational Response
step_4.hint: Another way to see the situation? What facts contradict this thought?

step_5.title: Outcome
step_5.hint: What emotion now? Intensity (0–100)? Belief in the alternative thought (0–100)?

beck_columns.footer: Each record is stored locally on the patient's device. History is accessible at any time.

cognitive_distortions.label: Cognitive Distortions Detector
cognitive_distortions.description: Educational cards on thinking traps (all-or-nothing, overgeneralization, mind-reading…)

grounding.label: Grounding Techniques
grounding.description: 5-4-3-2-1 exercise to return to the present during dissociation or severe rumination.

rim.label: RIM – Mental Imagery Reprocessing
rim.footer: Mental Imagery Reprocessing (RIM) — Krakow et al., JAMA 2001 · Schmid et al., Psychother Psychosom 2021. Alternative scenario configured by the clinician, read-only for the patient.

fear_thermometer.label: Fear Thermometer
fear_thermometer.description: Measure and visualize your anxiety level (SUDs, 0-100).
fear_thermometer.footer: The patient measures their subjective distress level (SUDs, Wolpe 1969) before and after a coping strategy. Data stored locally. Raw 0–100 values are reviewed by the clinician to assess the effectiveness of exposures and strategies.

exposure_hierarchy.label: Exposure Hierarchy
exposure_hierarchy.description: Graduated exposure step builder for phobias and OCD.

breathing_techniques.label: Breathing Techniques
breathing_techniques.description: Visual guide to box breathing and cardiac coherence with local counter.

field_1.label: Cardiac Coherence
field_1.detail: Lehrer & Gevirtz (2014) — Grade B · 5s inhale / 5s exhale · 5 min recommended

field_2.label: Diaphragmatic Breathing
field_2.detail: HAS, Conrad et al. (2007) — Grade B · 4s inhale / 7s exhale · 5 min recommended

field_3.label: Box Breathing
field_3.detail: VA/DoD Guidelines — Grade C · 4-4-4-4 · 4 min recommended

field_4.label: 4-7-8 Technique
field_4.detail: Expert consensus — Grade C · 4s-7s-8s · sleep onset, anxiety crisis · 3 min

field_5.label: Mindful Breathing
field_5.detail: MBCT Segal et al. (2002) — Grade A depressive relapse · 4-1-6-1 · 10 min recommended

field_6.label: Session History
field_6.detail: Technique used + actual duration — raw data, no interpretation

breathing_techniques.footer: Animated guide at a fixed pace — no biofeedback, no sensors. Session history (technique + duration) is accessible. MDR 2017/745 compliance: no algorithmic interpretation.

cognitive_saturation.label: Cognitive Saturation
cognitive_saturation.description: Rapid repetition exercise to interrupt ruminations.

craving_journal.label: Craving Journal
craving_journal.description: Log the urge intensity (0-10), the trigger, and the avoidance strategy used.

decisional_balance.label: Decisional Balance
decisional_balance.description: Motivational interviewing tool: weighing the pros and cons of a behaviour change.
decisional_balance.footer: Each argument is weighted 1 to 5 stars (importance). Dynamic motivation gauge: compares the Change score vs. the Status Quo score.

motivational_balance.label: Motivational Balance
motivational_balance.description: Motivational Interviewing (Miller & Rollnick): Prochaska stages, importance/confidence rulers 0-10, values balance.

gad7.label: GAD-7
gad7.description: 7-item generalized anxiety questionnaire (Spitzer et al., 2006). Score 0–21.

snap_iv.label: SNAP-IV
snap_iv.description: ADHD screening for children/adolescents — 26 items, 3 subscales.

asrs6.label: ASRS v1.1 — Screener
asrs6.description: Adult ADHD rapid screening — 6-item Part A (Kessler et al., 2005).

asrs18.label: ASRS v1.1 — Full Assessment
asrs18.description: Full adult ADHD assessment — 18 items Parts A+B (Kessler et al., 2005).

notifications.note_placeholder: E.g.: Remember to do it before the session…

notes.mic_error_not_supported: Your browser does not support audio recording.
notes.mic_error_permission: Microphone access denied. Check your browser permissions.
notes.mic_error_too_large: Recording too long. Please record shorter segments.
notes.mic_error_network: Network error during transcription. Check your connection.
notes.mic_error_recorder: Recording error. The recording has been stopped.
notes.mic_error_disconnected: Microphone disconnected. The recording has been stopped.

agenda.title: Schedule
agenda.subtitle: Configure your availability and manage appointments.
agenda.no_rules: No time slots defined. Start by configuring your availability.
agenda.empty_week: No slots configured for this week.

editor.title: My availability
editor.auto_confirm_hint: When a patient books online, the appointment is automatically confirmed

appointment.error_no_date: Please enter the date and time.
appointment.error_date_order: End time must be after start time.
appointment.pending_hint: the appointment will be pending confirmation.
appointment.practitioner_creates_hint: The patient will receive a notification and must confirm this appointment.

dispensaire.title: Clinical Library
dispensaire.subtitle: Library of validated questionnaires. Send them directly to your patients.
dispensaire.empty_category: No scales available in this category yet.
dispensaire.empty_soon: New questionnaires will be added soon.
dispensaire.no_patients: No patients in your list yet.
